tif

Class TiffDatePatcher

  • java.lang.Object
    • tif.TiffDatePatcher

  • public final class TiffDatePatcher
extends java.lang.Object
    Performs in-place "surgical patching" of specific timestamp entries. It allows for the modification of EXIF ASCII dates, GPS timestamps (stored as binary rationals), and XMP XML packets by overwriting the raw bytes at their existing file offsets.
    Since:
    15 February 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Constructor Detail

      • TiffDatePatcher

        private TiffDatePatcher()
        Default constructor is unsupported and will always throw an exception.
        Throws:
        java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException - to indicate that instantiation is not supported

    • Method Detail

      • patchAllDates

        public static void patchAllDates(java.nio.file.Path imagePath,
                                 java.nio.file.attribute.FileTime newDate,
                                 boolean xmpDump)
                          throws java.io.IOException
        Patches all identified metadata dates within the TIF file.

        Iterates through the IFD chain in reverse order to identify and process entries containing EXIF or XMP payloads. Input FileTime is interpreted using the ZoneId.systemDefault().

        Parameters:
        imagePath - the Path to the TIF to be patched
        newDate - the new timestamp to apply
        xmpDump - if true, exports the raw XMP buffer to a file for verification
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the TIFF structure is corrupt or the file is read-only

      • processExifSegment

        private static void processExifSegment(ImageRandomAccessWriter writer,
                                       DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry,
                                       java.time.ZonedDateTime zdt)
                                throws java.io.IOException
        Patches ASCII-formatted date tags in-place.

        Handles standard EXIF date tags, i.e, DateTimeOriginal, etc and GPSDateStamp. GPS tags are automatically coerced to UTC. The value is null-terminated and padded or truncated to fit the original slotWidthLimit defined by the IFD entry count.

        Parameters:
        writer - the active binary writer
        entry - the IFD entry targeting an ASCII field
        zdt - the target date and time
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the write operation fails

      • processExifGpsTimeStamp

        private static void processExifGpsTimeStamp(ImageRandomAccessWriter writer,
                                            DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry,
                                            java.time.ZonedDateTime zdt)
                                     throws java.io.IOException
        Patches the binary GPS time-stamp in-place using a sequence of three 64-bit rational numbers.

        Per the EXIF specification, the GPSTimeStamp tag (Tag 0x0007) consists of three RATIONAL values representing UTC hours, minutes, and seconds. Each rational occupies 8 bytes (a 4-byte unsigned integer numerator and a 4-byte unsigned integer denominator).

        It automatically converts the provided ZonedDateTime to the UTC zone to maintain compliance with GPS metadata standards, regardless of the local system's timezone settings.

        Parameters:
        writer - the writer used to perform the surgical binary modification
        entry - the DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD targeting the GPS_TIME_STAMP tag
        zdt - the target date and time to be converted and encoded
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs or the file offset is unreachable

      • processXmpSegment

        private static void processXmpSegment(ImageRandomAccessWriter writer,
                                      DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry,
                                      java.time.ZonedDateTime zdt,
                                      boolean xmpDump)
                               throws java.io.IOException
        Performs a binary-safe search and replace within an XMP XML packet. *

        It calculates physical byte offsets based on UTF-8 encoding rather than character indices. This prevents "positional drift" when the XML contains multi-byte characters (e.g., Unicode symbols or BOM).

        Only values with a sufficient slotByteWidth (minimum 10 bytes) are patched to prevent structure corruption.

        Parameters:
        writer - the active binary writer
        entry - the IFD entry containing the TagIFD_Baseline#IFD_XML_PACKET
        zdt - the replacement date
        xmpDump - indicates if the pre-patch buffer should be dumped to disk
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if binary seek or write fails
